title: Codex 2026 Prompting and Repository Practice · Contract Workshop
summary: Apply current Codex guidance across CLI, IDE, app, planning, and repository instructions. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Codex-specific prompt and AGENTS.md package while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.
Use Plan mode or an interview when requirements are fuzzy or choices are consequential.
Place recurring build, test, lint, review, and boundary guidance in the nearest applicable AGENTS.md.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.

content.text: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Codex Four Parts, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a Codex-specific prompt and AGENTS.md package.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Codex requires a rigid magic prompt syntax. This fails the Codex Four Parts contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Overview (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls success criteria, empirical evaluation, and the living Claude prompt-engineering workflow.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-codex-review-verify-2y0om3" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Codex Review Verify into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-codex-review-verify-2y0om3

Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Codex Review Verify condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Codex Review Verify applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prompt Codex to test, inspect the diff, review risk, and report evidence before completion.
